scope: Pass B full-surface expansion, re-reviewed against the live v2 resource-group documentation pages (api-group-page, api-group-space, api-group-label, api-group-comment, api-group-content-properties, api-group-custom-content, api-group-task, api-group-version, api-group-whiteboard, fetched 2026-07-04): 9 read streams (the 5 wave1-pilot streams retained, plus footer_comments/inline_comments/tasks/custom_content) and 6 write actions covering page/blogpost/comment lifecycle create+update. Every endpoint below is covered_by a stream/write action XOR excluded with a real category and reason (no blanket out_of_scope bucket). Exclusions favor breadth of real syncable content objects over: per-content-id-only sub-resource reads that have no global/cross-space list shape and would require a fan_out parent-id source this bundle does not have config for (ancestors, children, descendants, ~14 separate content-properties path families, ~12 separate version-history path families, smart-links/embeds, databases, folders, classification-levels, redactions, likes); single-item detail GETs that duplicate an already-covered list stream's record shape (duplicate_of); irreversible/destructive deletes with no reverse-ETL use case (destructive_admin); site/instance admin surfaces (admin-key, app-properties, data-policies, space-permissions, space-permission-transition, space-roles, user) that are configuration/access-control snapshots, not syncable business objects (non_data_endpoint); and whiteboard/database/folder/embed creation (canvas/binary/container-shaped payloads with no meaningful declarative record schema for this connector's purpose, out_of_scope).

excluded.reason: space creation is a workspace-provisioning admin action, not a reverse-ETL record mutation; no update/delete counterpart exists in v2 either (spaces are immutable via this API once created)
single-space detail; identical record shape to the spaces list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
space-scoped page list; identical record shape to the global pages stream, itself filterable by space-id; not modeled as a second stream
space-scoped blogpost list; identical record shape to the global blogposts stream
space-scoped label list; identical record shape to the global labels stream
space-content-scoped label list; identical record shape to the global labels stream
space-scoped custom-content list; identical record shape to the global custom_content stream
single-page detail; identical record shape to the pages list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
irreversible content delete, no reverse-ETL use case for this port
narrow single-field title rename fully subsumed by update_page's whole-record PUT; a separate write action for one field of an already-covered mutation adds no capability
label-scoped page list; identical record shape to the global pages stream
